channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Pro-worker AI,' streaming fatalities, and other fascinating new economic studies
item.description: From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.
item.content:encoded:
From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.

(Image credit: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Live Nation's settlement with DOJ still isn't a done deal
item.description: Live Nation has reached a settlement with the Department of Justice after a years-long antitrust battle. What could this mean for the broader live entertainment industry?
item.content:encoded: Live Nation has reached a settlement with the Department of Justice after a years-long antitrust battle. What could this mean for the broader live entertainment industry?
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: World shares tumble as Iran war pushes crude prices over $110 a barrel
item.description: World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.
item.content:encoded:
World shares tumbled on Monday, with Japan's benchmark Nikkei 225 index plunging more than 5%, after oil prices spiked at nearly $120 a barrel.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Crude oil prices swing wildly as the Iran war stretches on
item.description: Brent crude reached its highest price since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. Gasoline prices in the U.S. are expected to continue to rise.
item.content:encoded:
Brent crude reached its highest price since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. Gasoline prices in the U.S. are expected to continue to rise.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: OpenAI robotics leader resigns over concerns about Pentagon AI deal
item.description: A senior member of OpenAI's robotics team said guardrails around certain AI uses were not sufficiently defined before OpenAI announced an agreement with the Pentagon.
item.content:encoded:
A senior member of OpenAI's robotics team said guardrails around certain AI uses were not sufficiently defined before OpenAI announced an agreement with the Pentagon.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: U.S. Judge says Kari Lake broke law in overseeing Voice of America
item.description: He declared all of Lake's actions over the past year to be null and void, including the layoffs of more than 1,000 journalists and staffers.
item.content:encoded:
He declared all of Lake's actions over the past year to be null and void, including the layoffs of more than 1,000 journalists and staffers.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.
item.content:encoded:
Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: No lawsuits required: U.S. Customs is working on a system to refund tariffs
item.description: U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The U.S. unexpectedly loses 92,000 jobs, adding to worries about the economy
item.description: The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.
item.content:encoded:
The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: 2 young billionaires are behind the prediction market boom. They hate each other
item.description: The 20-something billionaires who run Kalshi and Polymarket are battling it out to be the top prediction market company. Observers and former insiders say the feud is just heating up.
item.content:encoded: The 20-something billionaires who run Kalshi and Polymarket are battling it out to be the top prediction market company. Observers and former insiders say the feud is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn
